checks.a_analysis: The JSON output contains technical placeholders like 'Content from pages 11-21 could not be converted', which are clearly section labels indicating pipeline errors rather than attempts to hallucinate document content. All other body text appears to be derived directly from the source PDF.

All contact information, including the Part C Coordinator's email (jill.singer@dhhs.nc.gov), phone number (919-707-5535), and various beearly.nc.gov URLs, were verified against the source PDF and found to be correct.

A significant portion of the document is missing from the JSON output. Specifically, Pages 12 through 29 are absent. This range contains the entirety of Indicators 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7, including historical data, FFY 2015 performance data, required actions, and correction of noncompliance sections for each. The JSON includes placeholder sections explicitly stating these pages were not converted.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 12-29 containing Indicator 3 (Early Childhood Outcomes), Indicator 4 (Family Involvement), Indicator 5 (Child Find Birth to One), Indicator 6 (Child Find Birth to Three), and Indicator 7 (45-day timeline).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON jumps from Indicator 2 (ending page 11) directly to Indicator 8 (starting page 30), with placeholders '[Pages 11-21 unavailable]' and '[Pages 21-31 unavailable]'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Between element index heading-40 and heading-41 (reading_order 8 and 9)

checks.a_analysis: For the pages that were converted, the tables appear to be complete. The Indicator 1 Historical Data table correctly captures the columns for FFY 2004-2014 and the corresponding target/actual data rows. The Indicator 8A Historical Data table also preserves all relevant data points. While many tables are missing due to the entire page omissions (Indicators 3-7), this is captured under C3.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The converted sections (Intro, Indicators 1, 2, 8, 9, 10, 11, and Certification) maintain high fidelity to the source text. No significant paragraphs or content blocks within these converted pages appear to be omitted.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Numerical values and specific percentages (e.g., FFY 2015 Indicator 1 data of 99.12%, Indicator 8A data of 98.70%) were cross-checked and found to be accurately transcribed. Meaning is preserved throughout the converted text.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion results show several sections that appear redundant, such as the recurring 'Indicator 8' criteria list (A, B, C) in sections 9, 10, and 11. However, a review of the source PDF confirms that these lists are intentionally repeated on the start of each subsection (pages 31, 36, 41) in the original document. No evidence of pipeline-introduced duplication was found.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and broken across major sections. While section titles are defined in the JSON metadata for every section, the corresponding text from the source document is missing from the 'elements' array as navigable heading elements in several cases (e.g., sections 2, 6, 9, 10, 12, and 13). For example, Section 9 (Indicator 8A) begins with 'Baseline Data: 2005' instead of the Indicator title. Conversely, Section 14 correctly includes its title as heading-45. This inconsistency prevents logical navigation via screen readers.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 31 heading: 'Indicator 8A: Early Childhood Transition'; Page 36 heading: 'Indicator 8B: Early Childhood Transition'; Page 41 heading: 'Indicator 8C: Early Childhood Transition'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 9 index 0: 'Baseline Data: 2005' (The main indicator title is missing from the elements array). Section 10 index 0: 'Baseline Data: 2005'.

checks.a_analysis: Tables were analyzed for structural fidelity, including the complex historical data tables (e.g., page 4) and the compliance data tables (e.g., page 33). The output correctly identifies header rows and preserves the column structure (e.g., the 12-column historical data table in Section 2 is correctly rendered). No corrupted table structures were detected.

The reading order diverges materially from the source document due to the handling of missing pages. Approximately 20 pages of the original document (Indicators 3 through 7) are missing from their logical sequential positions. While placeholders are provided (Sections 15 and 16), they are appended to the end of the JSON (reading_order 15 and 16) following the conclusion of the document (Section 14, page 60). This breaks the logical flow of information, as content from page 11 jumps immediately to page 31.
checks.b_evidence_source: Sequential document flow from Page 1 to Page 60.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON sequence is Section 8 (Page 11 content) followed by Section 9 (Page 31 content). The missing content placeholders for pages 11-31 are placed at the very end of the file (Sections 15 and 16) instead of between sections 8 and 9.

checks.a_analysis: Lists were checked for structural integrity. The list of indicators on page 1 and the transition criteria (A, B, C) on page 31 are correctly identified as 'unordered' and 'ordered' lists respectively. The conversion preserves the relationship between items.

The output successfully captures external government URLs and contact links found in the source. For example, the .gov PDF links on page 3 and the mailto link for Jill Singer on page 60 are correctly identified and preserve the source URI.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-page PDF structure is converted into a single, logically ordered scrolling page. Section headers (Introduction, General Supervision System, Indicators 1-11) are present and clearly delineated. Tables and narrative text blocks appear in the same relative sequence as they do in the source document. The addition of a navigation landmark/table of contents at the top is a standard accessibility feature and does not constitute a layout defect.

All tables from the source PDF, including complex ones like the Indicator 3 Historical Data (page 12) and the Outcome A/B/C tables (pages 13-14), are rendered correctly in HTML. Rows and columns are properly aligned, borders are present to distinguish cells, and headers are visually distinct. The data remains legible and structurally intact in the HTML format.

The source document contains very few images, mostly restricted to UI elements like radio buttons or checkmarks (e.g., pages 5, 13, 18). These elements are appropriately represented in the HTML output. No meaningful content images (such as charts, logos, or diagrams) present in the PDF are missing from the HTML rendering.

The document does not contain any visually duplicated body content. While section headings and titles appear in the navigation area (TOC) and again in the body, this is an intentional accessibility feature for document navigation (landmarks) and is not a content duplication defect. Paraphs, tables, and specific data points appear only once in the body content flow.
